reasoning: The NBER declares recessions based on broad-based declines in economic activity, considering GDP, employment, real income, and consumer spending. Available data confirms robust US economic performance throughout 2023 and 2024. For example, GDP grew at 2.5% in 2023, and sequential quarters in 2024 showed strong growth (e.g., Q3 at ~2.8% and Q4 at 2.4%). While labor market conditions showed some cooling mid-year, broad economic activity never contracted. Given that it is currently August 2025, the initial and revised economic data for 2023–2024 are fully known and uniformly positive, and Manifold priced this market at ~1% in July 2025, there is virtually no chance the NBER will retrospectively declare a recession for that period by the end of 2025. Per system instructions to respect a 0.05 minimum, I set my forecast to 5%.
